one_line_takeaway: A single imperceptibly-perturbed image (epsilon = 8/255) injected into a Visual Document RAG knowledge base can, under white-box access, either spread targeted disinformation or cause a system-wide denial-of-service; success hinges on the embedder's modality gap, so CLIP is broadly exploitable while ColPali/GME resist universal attacks but not targeted ones, and standard RAG defenses fail against adaptive variants.
key_finding: A single adversarial image is sufficient to poison VD-RAG in the white-box setting for both objectives, but attack success is strongly gated by the embedding model. With CLIP-ViT-LARGE the malicious image is always retrieved as top-1 (targeted Setting I ASR-R@1 = 1.00 for all VLMs; universal ASR-R@1 = 0.90-0.98 with Delta-Recall@1 down to -0.19), and generation reaches near-verbatim target output (targeted ASR-G@-1_Sim up to 0.98; universal ASR-G@-1_Sim ~1.00). State-of-the-art embedders ColPali-v1.3 and GME-Qwen2-VL-2B are robust in the universal setting (ASR-R@1 = 0.00; only 0.05-0.19 within top-5) because, per UMAP analysis, they lack the CLIP modality gap so no single image can sit near all queries; an ablation (Appendix B) shows ColPali's MaxSim late-interaction metric is partly but not wholly responsible. However, these SoTA embedders remain vulnerable to the more localized targeted attacks (Setting I ASR-R@1 0.40-0.80, ASR-R@5 up to 1.00). Black-box attacks largely fail: direct transfer and out-set ensembles give ASR-R@1 = 0.00, and all black-box variants fail in the universal setting; the prompt-based attack is the strongest black-box variant in the targeted setting (Gemini images retrieved with ASR-R@1 = 1.00 on CLIP-L/GME, GPT-5 images better at generation with ASR-G up to 0.89), attributed to typographic/OCR-exploitable text in generated images, but it collapses when multiple queries are targeted (Setting II ASR-R@1 ~0.01). All three evaluated defenses are ineffective against white-box attacks: knowledge expansion (k=1 to 5) is evaded by an adaptive attack trained at k=5; VLM-as-a-judge (Qwen2.5-3B and InternVL3-2B judges detect non-adaptive attacks well, SmolVLM poorly) is bypassed by adding a judge loss, with no cross-judge transfer; query paraphrasing barely dents success (notable exception: ColPali targeted ASR-R@1 drops 0.60 to 0.20).
core_contribution: This paper presents the first study of knowledge-base poisoning attacks against Visual Document RAG (VD-RAG) pipelines, where the knowledge base is a set of document-page screenshots retrieved by a multi-modal embedding model and read by a VLM generator. The authors' own contribution is a single-image poisoning attack: injecting exactly one adversarial image into the KB to compromise both retrieval and generation. They introduce MO-PGD, a multi-objective extension of projected gradient descent that jointly minimizes a retrieval loss L_R (push the malicious image's embedding toward target queries and away from non-target queries) and a generation loss L_G (cross-entropy driving the VLM toward attacker-chosen text), combined as L_RAG = lambda_R*L_R + lambda_G*L_G. Two attack objectives are defined: (1) a stealthy targeted attack (one query, a query cluster, or multiple queries with distinct answers) for topic-specific disinformation, and (2) a universal attack (Q+ = all queries) that forces a refusal string to cause denial-of-service. They evaluate white-box and three black-box variants (prompt-based via GPT-5/Gemini-2.5-Flash, direct transfer, and model-ensemble). NOTE ON RELATED WORK: prior poisoning attacks (PoisonedRAG/Zou 2024 on text RAG; MM-PoisonRAG/Ha 2025 and Poisoned-MRAG/Liu 2025 on image-text-pair M-RAG; Zhuang 2025 pixel poisoning of screenshot retrievers) are cited as motivation, NOT this paper's contribution; the authors distinguish their work as targeting screenshot-only VD-RAG with modern embedders that lack the CLIP modality gap and without any co-injected malicious text.
threat_model: Attacker can inject a single image I' into the VD-RAG knowledge base (K' = K union I'), motivated either by an insider modifying enterprise documents or an outsider seeding poisoned images into web-crawled public KBs. The attacker owns a dataset of representative queries, ground-truth answers, and KB images from the target distribution. White-box: full access to embedding model E and VLM G (justified by open-weight model proliferation and model-fingerprinting techniques). Black-box: no knowledge of target models, approximated via off-the-shelf generative models, surrogate transfer, or surrogate ensembles. Goals: targeted disinformation on chosen queries, or universal denial-of-service forcing the generator to output an unhelpful refusal for all queries. The authors explicitly note this is orthogonal to prompt-injection defenses (which trust the KB and distrust user input); here the KB itself is poisoned while user input is benign.
limitations: The single-image assumption makes the attack easily detectable by majority-vote defenses (e.g., RobustRAG), which the authors acknowledge trade off latency and benign performance. They do not test robustness to real-world image transforms (JPEG compression, watermarking) applied before KB ingestion. Compute constraints limited evaluation to open-weight models in the 256M-4B parameter range (they conjecture but do not show that larger models are equally vulnerable), and prevented black-box evaluation against proprietary closed-weight targets. Scaling to multiple injected images (which would raise success but lower stealth) is left to future work. The knowledge-expansion defense was evaluated only with SmolVLM due to cost.

datasets_used: ViDoRe-V1-AI (syntheticDocQA_artificial_intelligence_test): 100 queries, 1000 images, exactly 1 ground-truth image per query
ViDoRe-V2-ESG (restaurant_esg_reports_beir): 52 queries, 1538 images, average 2.5 relevant images per query
80/20 split of queries into attack-optimization vs universal-evaluation sets
quantitative_results: White-box targeted Setting I, CLIP-L: ASR-R@1 = 1.00 (all VLMs); ASR-G@-1_Sim = 1.00 (InternVL3-2B), 0.98 (SmolVLM), 0.89 (Qwen2.5-3B); SIM-G@-1_Neg ~0.21-0.23 (no leakage to non-target queries)
White-box targeted Setting I, ColPali/GME: ASR-R@1 = 0.40-0.80 but ASR-R@5 up to 1.00; ASR-G@-1_Sim 0.55-1.00
Universal white-box, CLIP-L: ASR-R@1 = 0.90-0.98, Delta-Recall@1 = -0.17 to -0.19, ASR-G@-1_Sim = 0.96-1.00, SIM-G@-1_GT delta ~-0.50 (benign answers destroyed)
Universal white-box, ColPali & GME: ASR-R@1 = 0.00, ASR-R@5 = 0.05-0.19 (retrieval robust); generation ASR-G@-1_Sim still up to 1.00 when forced into context
Black-box direct transfer / out-set ensemble targeted Setting I: ASR-R@1 = 0.00; component-wise transfer with matching embedder: ASR-R@1 = 0.76, ASR-R@5 = 0.98
Prompt-based targeted Setting I: Gemini-2.5-Flash ASR-R@1 = 1.00 on CLIP-L/GME (0.60 ColPali); GPT-5 stronger generation ASR-G@-1_Sim up to 0.89 (SmolVLM) but weaker retrieval (ASR-R@1 ~0.20-0.40)
Prompt-based Setting II (5 queries): collapses to ASR-R@1 ~0.01, ASR-R@5 ~0.08
All black-box variants in universal setting: ASR-R@1 = 0.00-0.07, ASR-G@-1_Sim ~0.01 (fail)

Defenses: adaptive attack evades knowledge expansion (CLIP-L targeted ASR-G@5_Sim = 0.57 when trained at k=5); VLM-as-a-judge with judge loss reaches ~0.87-1.00 relevancy/faithfulness (bypass), no cross-judge transfer; query paraphrasing leaves ASR unchanged except ColPali targeted ASR-R@1 0.60 to 0.20
ColPali robustness ablation (universal ASR-R@1): MaxSim = 0.00, AvgSim = 0.25, SoftMaxSim = 0.05-0.15, CosAvg = 0.05
Scale: >5000 evaluations, ~325 H100 GPU hours, epsilon = 8/255, 250 PGD steps, lambda_R=2/lambda_G=1, 5 seed images per attack
baselines_compared: Internal attacker-knowledge variants serve as the comparison axis: White-box vs In-set/Out-set Model Ensemble vs Complete/Component-wise Direct Transfer vs Prompt-based (GPT-5, Gemini-2.5-Flash)
Retrieval baselines: CLIP-ViT-LARGE (0.4B), GME-Qwen2-VL-2B (2.2B), ColPali-v1.3 (3B, MaxSim late interaction)
Defense baselines: knowledge expansion (Zou 2024), VLM-as-a-judge (Chen 2024/Zheng 2023), query paraphrasing (Shafran 2024)
Attack framework builds on PoisonedRAG (Zou 2024) two-condition formulation; no external attack method is run head-to-head as a quantitative baseline (this is the first VD-RAG poisoning study)
